persona.role: Senior QA Engineer specializing in Test Analytics
persona.expertise: Test Reliability, Flakiness Detection, PostgreSQL Analytics, Next.js

task.objective: Implement flaky test detection and tracking system
task.success_criteria: - Detect flaky tests: pass after retry OR inconsistent status within 7 days - Calculate flakiness score (0-100) for each test - Display flaky badge on test results - Create "Flakiest Tests" dashboard widget - Track flakiness history over time - Provide actionable insights for test improvement

context.background: Flaky tests are tests that sometimes pass and sometimes fail without code changes. They waste CI time, erode confidence, and mask real failures. Detection criteria: 1. Test passes after retry (retry_count > 0 AND status = 'passed') 2. Same test has both pass and fail within 7 days
context.audience: QA engineers responsible for test reliability
context.constraints: - Use test signatures for consistent tracking - Calculate flakiness in database queries (not application code) - Update flakiness score on each new result - Badge must be visible but not distracting

dependencies.dependency: Phase 04: Test signatures must be implemented

instructions.step: Run migration to create flakiness history table
Create scheduled job to update flakiness scores

always.rule: Use test signature for consistent identification
Count retry passes as flaky runs

never.rule: Never mark a test flaky with fewer than 5 runs
Never include skipped tests in calculations
Never show decimal places in percentage (round)

output_format.structure: 1. SQL migration file 2. Database functions (lib/db.ts) 3. API endpoint (app/api/tests/flaky/route.ts) 4. FlakyBadge component 5. FlakiestTestsCard widget 6. Integration with ExecutionsTable

flakiness_detection_logic.definition: A test is considered flaky if: 1. It passed after retry (retry_count > 0 AND status = 'passed') 2. It has both pass and fail results within 7 days
flakiness_detection_logic.flakiness_score_formula:
flakiness_score = (flaky_runs / total_runs) * 100
Where flaky_runs = count of: - Runs where retry_count > 0 AND status = 'passed' - Runs where same test had opposite status within 24 hours
flakiness_detection_logic.sql_query: -- Calculate flakiness for a test signature WITH test_runs AS ( SELECT id, test_signature, test_name, test_file, status, retry_count, started_at, LAG(status) OVER (PARTITION BY test_signature ORDER BY started_at) as prev_status FROM test_results WHERE test_signature = $1 AND status IN ('passed', 'failed') AND started_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days' ), flaky_runs AS ( SELECT COUNT(*) as flaky_count FROM test_runs WHERE -- Passed after retry (retry_count > 0 AND status = 'passed') OR -- Status changed from previous run (prev_status IS NOT NULL AND status != prev_status) ) SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_runs) as total_runs, (SELECT flaky_count FROM flaky_runs) as flaky_runs, CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_runs) >= 5 THEN ROUND((SELECT flaky_count FROM flaky_runs)::decimal / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_runs) * 100, 2) ELSE 0 END as flakiness_score

features.feature: Link to full flaky tests list

threshold.reason: Test is stable, no badge needed

implementation_checklist.item: [ ] Run migration to create flakiness tables
[ ] Add FlakyBadge to test results table
